title: arenaria fontqueri subsp. hispanica
intro: Arenaria fontqueri subsp. hispanica, also known as Spanish sandwort, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Spanish sandwort include its potential to treat various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Traditional herbal preparations of Spanish sandwort include infusions and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health concerns and promote overall well-being.
